ideal_answer_shape: Short answer states the likely implementation location or answer first.
Strongest evidence lists the best file and line references before supporting refs.
Supporting evidence is separated from direct implementation or focused test evidence.
Tests and proof identify focused tests or state that no bounded test evidence was found.
Gaps and confidence are explicit when evidence is incomplete or indirect.
must_have_ranking_rules: Exact behavior evidence beats broad keyword matches.
Implementation and focused test evidence outrank documentation, examples, snapshots, and broad references.
Direct call-path or handler-branch evidence outranks path-sorted adjacent source matches.
Specific file and line references must be inspectable without another search.
Weak or missing evidence must be labeled as a gap instead of presented as proof.
negative_cases: A broad source keyword match is listed before an exact focused behavior test.
A broad line match appears before an exact symbol or behavior line in the same file.
Request-flow evidence is ordered by path instead of direct handler or branch relevance.
The answer lists files without relevance labels or gap boundaries.
The answer claims tests exist when only nearby tests or keyword matches were found.
rubric: Ranking quality and demotion of weak matches: 20 points.
Specificity and inspectability of file and line refs: 15 points.
Correctness discipline and no unsupported inference: 10 points.
